step_by_step_thinking:
Agree — The key point is that improved survival due to better treatments leads to an increase in the number of long-term survivors who remain diagnosed throughout their lives. While the rate of new diagnoses (incidence) stays constant because underlying causes like UV radiation aren't altered, each patient now survives much longer, thus increasing overall prevalence. 

Answer choice: B

I agree. Although the treatment improves survival without altering risk factors, prolonged duration of illness means more individuals live with the condition simultaneously, thereby increasing prevalence while incidence remains unchanged due to stable etiology.  
Answer choice: B
